Part of a series on
'''Medicine'''
[[File:ABHOLOGOPROPER.png|center|150px|link=]]
'''''Anomaliae Corporis'''''
{| style="width: 100%; margin-top:1em; text-align:left; border-radius: 1px;" |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Systematic designation''' | style="width: 70%;" | Phenomenon 303 ([[The Database|Database]]) |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Other names''' | style="width: 70%;" | Anatomical Anomalies |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Specialty''' | style="width: 70%;" | Xeniatrology |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Usual onset''' | style="width: 70%;" | Upon [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/No-Clipping?redirect=no no-clippage] into the Backrooms, at birth |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Diagnostic method''' | style="width: 70%;" | Based on symptoms, condition-specific methods |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Treatment''' | style="width: 70%;" | None |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Frequency''' | style="width: 70%;" | Rare, 0.2% ([[Backrooms Health Organisation|B.H.P.A.]]) |}
'''Phenomenon 303''', known alternatively as '''''Anomaliae Corporis''''' or '''Anatomical Anomalies''', is an umbrella term for a variety of xeniatrological anomalies which may rarely affect wanderers upon their first entry of the Backrooms, or — in the case of Wakeborn individuals — at the time of their birth.[[Asclepius Ang|Ang, Asclepius L.]] (2023). ''The Effects of Anatomical Anomalies in Their Variably Benign or Malignant Forms: Part One of a Tripartite Study''. Egremond & Roth.Sallow, Godfrey H. (2019) [1947]. ''Medical Peculiarities of the Wake''. Greyscale House. These peculiarities manifest in many different shapes and forms, many of which do not bear the least semblance to each other; the only similarity is that all of these oddities warp, transform or alter the victim's anatomy in a certain fashion, whether for good or for ill, though typically being unexplainable by orthodox medicine. Each individual instance of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' has been so minor, or occurred in such a little amount of people, that they have been determined to all be grouped under the name of a singular [[Phenomenon List|phenomenon]].Ruxton, Peter (2021, January 8). "''Anomaliae Corporis'': What Is It, and Why Should You Care?" ''The New Londoner''. In recent years, attention has been drawn to ''Anomaliae Corporis'' in its various forms under the Anomalia Awareness Research Initiative, and various medicinal divisions like the Department of Biomedical Research of the [[Backrooms Red Cross]] and the An Apple a Day medical team of the [[M.E.G.|Major Explorer Group]] are conducting ongoing research regarding this phenomenon.Reighnolds, Daniel (2020, December 13). "Anomalia Research Initiative Makes a Righteous Return!" [[Backrooms Today|''Backrooms, Today!'']] The [[Backrooms_Health_Organisation|Backrooms Health Practitioner Authority]] has dedicated much intensive research toward investigation of the causes, effects and possible cures of these anomalies, with hope that those affected with the phenomenon's more malignant strains may someday be alleviated of their pitiful conditions.Diggs, Charlotte (2020). "Rekindling the Fire: The Post-Flicker Return of the Anomalia Awareness Research Initiative". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 29(13). == Terminology == The term ''Anomaliae Corporis'', meaning "anomalies of the body" in Latin, was first coined in 1947 by medical scholar Dr Godfrey Sallow.After being subject to a serious instance of time dilation shortly after his coinage of the term, Dr Sallow found himself in the 21st century and subsequently joined the [[Level 4|Society of the Red Cross]], where he still works to this day. Its original meaning was in reference to any of the strange medical conditions which could be contracted in the Backrooms, including pathogenic diseases such as [[Sanguine Festivus]], [[Entity 8|Egremond Syndrome]] and [[Hydrolitis Plague|Hydromonia]], as well as mental illnesses such as [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]]. As better documentation of these more major ailments developed, the definition of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' has been restricted to denoting a class of contracted-upon-noclip, typically permanent conditions which affect the physical anatomy of an individual. As originally dictated by Dr Sallow, the term '''''corpora anomaliarum'''''SINGULAR: ''corpus anomaliarum'' ("anomalous bodies") was used to refer to individuals affected with ''Anomaliae Corporis'', but this name has fallen out of fashion in recent decades due to many viewing it as a derogatory term of commodifying ostracism. Sallow himself has recently stated that he strongly opposes the use of this term, and acknowledges that he was "wrong to coin such a derogatory name".Reighnolds, Daniel (2022, June 30). "Interview with Dr Godfrey Sallow: Time-Travelling Medico". [[Backrooms Today|''Backrooms, Today!'']]Quek, Patricia N. (2022, July 14). "The doctor from 1947, and his opinions on modern medicine". ''Wanderdom''. == Documented conditions == The following is a list of the various documented conditions attributed to ''Anomaliae Corporis''.
'''Cephalorrheximegaly'''
{| style="width: 100%; margin-top:1em; text-align:left; border-radius: 1px;" |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Other names''' | style="width: 70%;" | Cranial storm; Exploding Head Syndrome (EHS) |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Specialty''' | style="width: 70%;" | Neurology, otolaryngology, xeniatrology |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Symptoms''' | style="width: 70%;" | Swelling cranium |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Complications''' | style="width: 70%;" | Explosion of the head |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Diagnostic method''' | style="width: 70%;" | Based on symptoms, X-rays, MRI |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Deaths''' | style="width: 70%;" | Common |}
=== Cephalorrheximegaly === '''Cephalorrheximegaly''',Derived from ''cephalo-'' ("of the head"), ''-rrhexis'' ("rupture, burst") and ''-megaly'' ("abnormal enlargement"). also known as a '''cranial storm''' or colloquially as '''EHS (Exploding Head Syndrome)''',Not to be confused with the benign parasomnia of the same name. is an Anatomical Anomaly which involves the affected individual's head and skull to begin swelling at a measured rate from the moment they contract the condition (i.e. upon [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/No-Clipping?redirect=no no-clippage] or at birth), due to an anomalous pressure build-up within the cranium.Ostler, Wilhelm (2022). "Cephalorrheximegaly". ''The Xeniatrologist'', 9(2). Eventually, the individual's head will burst apart owing to the immense stress it is being put under, causing an immediate death due to massive cranial trauma; this can occur from anywhere between 2 months to 10 years after cephalorrheximegaly is first contracted.Greyscale House. Alongside thermoligohydria, cephalorrheximegaly is considered to be one of the most dangerous instances of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' known to medicine.Garcia, Emily (2026) [2020]. ''Horrors of the Wake: The Physical and the Psychological''. Egremond & Roth. Researchers in the [[Backrooms Red Cross]] and the [[Backrooms Health Organisation]] are currently scrambling to understand this phenomenon and its causes in greater detail.Queller, Johnathan (2025, March 16). "Why Do People's Heads Keep Exploding? A Deep Dive into EHS." ''Dimensional''. As of 2024, an approximate of 3,500 wanderers are known to have perished from a cranial storm.
'''Cryexia'''
{| style="width: 100%; margin-top:1em; text-align:left; border-radius: 1px;" |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Other names''' | style="width: 70%;" | Tepher; fimbulwinter |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Specialty''' | style="width: 70%;" | Neurology, physiology, xeniatrology |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Symptoms''' | style="width: 70%;" | Slowed heart rate, skin cold to touch |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Complications''' | style="width: 70%;" | Susceptibility to hyperthermia |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Diagnostic method''' | style="width: 70%;" | Based on symptoms, infrared thermal imaging, functional MRI |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Deaths''' | style="width: 70%;" | Rare directly (see ''Complications'') |}
=== Cryexia === '''Cryexia''',Derived from Ancient Greek ''κρύος'' ("ice"), on the pattern of "pyrexia" from ''πῦρ'' ("fire"). also known as '''tepher'''Derived from Ancient Greek ''τέφρᾱ'' ("ashes, as of the funeral pyre"), a cognate term with Latin ''febris'' ("fever"), whence came the English word "fever". or informally as '''fimbulwinter''', is an Anatomical Anomaly which involves the body temperature dropping below its normal range (typically between 24-31°C) due to changes in the hypothalamus,Sallow, Godfrey H. (2024). "How the Brain Relates to Cryexia". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 33(5). effectively the opposite of a regular fever. thus an affected person's skin will become cold to the touch of a normal individual, among other symptoms. The condition does not prevent hypothermia, and in fact increases risk of contracting hyperthermia.Rivers, Ethan P. (2022). ''Curses in Disguise''. Anemoiacore.Gale, Dorothy (2015). "Cryexia". ''The Xeniatrologist'', 2(8). Examples of cryexial individuals include the [[M.E.G.]] operative known as [[Rainy]], head of the Team Voyager exploration company, who is believed to be tepherous — although they have refused a diagnosis offered by the [[Level 4|Society of the Red Cross]].Reighnolds, Daniel (2019, November 21). "Interview with Rainy: Are They Really Immortal?" [[Backrooms Today|''Backrooms, Today!'']][[Rainy]] (2021) [2009]. ''All Power Comes with a Price: My Story''. Alpha & Omega.
'''Encephalomantiasis'''
{| style="width: 100%; margin-top:1em; text-align:left; border-radius: 1px;" |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Other names''' | style="width: 70%;" | Cerebral psychostasis; clarivoyance, telepathy, empathy, ESP, psychic powers, mind control |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Specialty''' | style="width: 70%;" | Neurology, parapsychology, psychoradiology, xeniatrology |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Symptoms''' | style="width: 70%;" | Resistance to achospineurotic conditions caused by neuron malfunction, such as [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]] |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Complications''' | style="width: 70%;" | Seizures, brain damage, increased exposure to potential cognitohazards |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Diagnostic method''' | style="width: 70%;" | Based on symptoms, psychoradiological imaging |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Deaths''' | style="width: 70%;" | Rare |}
=== Encephalomantiasis === '''Encephalomantiasis''',Derived from ''encephalo-'' ("of the brain") and Ancient Greek ''μαντεία'' ("prophecy, divination"). also known as '''cerebral psychostasis''' or by more colloquial terms including '''clairvoyance''', '''telepathy''' and '''ESP (Extrasensory Perception)''', is an achospineurotic''Achospineurotic:'' (OF A CONDITION) relating to or having been caused by psychic radiation from the Achospic Plane. Anatomical Anomaly which rewires the neurons in the brain to be more resistant to psychic radiation,[[Entity 444|Nightingale, Astrid F.]] (2019). ''Encephalomantiasis: What Doesn't Drive You Insane...'' [[User:MoonlightSerenity|Moonlight Serenity]]. typically making the affected individual less receptive toward such diseases as [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]].Tiresias, Prospero M. (2024) [2013]. ''Oracles and Psychics: Bending the Achospic Plane''. Almond Eye. It allows the cerebrum to filter, process and discard any psychic radiation which would otherwise interfere with its mental processes,Fury, Rubeus (2017). "Roth Syndrome". ''The Xeniatrologist'', 4(4). often subconsciously; so that the affected may perceive things undetectable to a regular individual, thus appearing to be "psychic", in layman's terms.[[User:TheDadams|Adams, Theodore D.]] (2014). ''Backrooms 101: A Wanderer's Toolbox''. Anemoiacore. Skilled encephalomantiatics may additionally use their condition to generate illusions or manipulate the thoughts and emotions of others, and rarely to create [[Phenomenon 9-A|thoughtformations]] at will; however, overstraining the brain's mental capacity to warp and redirect Achospic radiation may result in seizures or brain damage. Individuals such as [[Caius Taggert|Sir Caius Orpheus Taggert]][[Caius Taggert|Taggert, Caius O.]] (2017). ''Your Gracious Host (The Life and Times of the Party Host)''. Gaiety Books.Alastor (2020) [c. 1750]. ''[[I Met a Monster]]''. Oligarchus. and the serial killer [[Jack Umbra]],Larson, Amelia (2014). ''Friday, 13th October: My Encounter with the Pumpkinhead''. Greyscale House. as well as perhaps [[Clyde Nelson|Clyde Nelson, Esq.]],Carlson, Carlos C. (Director) ''et al.'', 2023. ''The Rise of the Hallowe'en Party'' [Documentary]. [[User:SmolSlasherSoul|Small Slasher Studios]].[[Caius Taggert|Taggert, Caius O.]] (2021). ''Dearly Beloved (My Friends on the Other Side)''. Gaiety Books. are believed to be cerebrally psychostatic, owing to their seeming ability to sense the thoughts and emotions of those whom they encounter.
'''Hyperlongevitism'''
{| style="width: 100%; margin-top:1em; text-align:left; border-radius: 1px;" |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Other names''' | style="width: 70%;" | Methulselah Syndrome; eternal youth |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Specialty''' | style="width: 70%;" | Gerontology/geriatrics, physiology, xeniatrology |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Symptoms''' | style="width: 70%;" | Cessation of ageing |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Complications''' | style="width: 70%;" | None |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Diagnostic method''' | style="width: 70%;" | Based on symptoms |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Deaths''' | style="width: 70%;" | Rare directly |}
=== Hyperlongevitism === '''Hyperlongevitism''', also known as '''Methuselah Syndrome''', is an Anatomical Anomaly which seemingly halts the ageing process entirely, thus making the affected person biologically immortal,Gale, Dorothy (2016). "Hyperlongevitism". The Xeniatrologist, 3(1). although the exact way in which this phenomenon functions is current the subject of much intensive research. Apparently, there is no set age at which an individual affected with hyperlongevitism stops ageing; various case studies reveal this point can be as low as 12 or as high as 95 years of age.Peh, Johnathan J. J. ''et al.'' (2024). "Methuselahs: They Aren't Always Old". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 33(4). The former case refers to a certain Josiah McGregor Howes of the [[Backroom Colonists]], who is known to have no-clipped from [[the Frontrooms]] into the [[Level 1|Parking Zone]] during the mid-1940s and has remained an adolescent ever since.Princeton, Lysander (2021). ''The Strangest Men Alive''. Almond Eye.Reighnolds, Daniel (2022, March 3). "Interview with Josiah Howes: Forever Young". [[Backrooms Today|''Backrooms, Today!'']] Doctors from the [[Backrooms Red Cross|Red Cross]] have attempted to transplant the organs of dying individuals affected by hyperlongevitism into the bodies of mundane test subjects, so as to experiment as to whether the condition can be transferred, but so far no traces of anomalous property have been detected after transplantation in organs such as the kidneys, the liver nor even the heart.Emily, Charlotte L. (2022). "The Effects of Transplant on Hyperlongevitism". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 31(9). It is thus believed that the condition is innate to the affected individual themself, although this has yet to be explicitly confirmed. [[The First]] is believed to have Methuselah Syndrome.Peterson, Lyra (2018). ''First Things First — How Is He so Old?'' Alpha & Omega.
'''Pathostasis'''
{| style="width: 100%; margin-top:1em; text-align:left; border-radius: 1px;" |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Other names''' | style="width: 70%;" | Xenostasis |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Specialty''' | style="width: 70%;" | Oncology, pathology, physiology, xeniatrology |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Symptoms''' | style="width: 70%;" | Pathogenic diseases confined to specific body parts |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Complications''' | style="width: 70%;" | Cancer, necrosis, paralysis |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Diagnostic method''' | style="width: 70%;" | Based on symptoms, blood testing |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Deaths''' | style="width: 70%;" | Moderate |}
=== Pathostasis === '''Pathostasis''',Derived from ''patho-'' ("disease") and ''-stasis'' ("stopping, maintaining"). also known as '''xenostasis''',Derived from ''xeno-'' ("foreign"). is an Anatomical Anomaly which causes the afflicted wanderer's body to confine any pathogenic condition it may contract to a specific part of the anatomy, usually one of the extremities, with no effect on the remainder of the body. In addition, pathostasis causes the body to develop an extreme resistance to any disease it develops, thus preventing the singular afflicted portion from infecting the rest of the anatomy.Gale, Dorothy (2021). "Pathostasis". ''The Xeniatrologist'', 8(5).Georgius, Joshua (2021, May 19). "Is pathostasis really all that good?" ''Wanderdom''. Due to this condition, there are several wanderers known to have a furred, canine limb owing to pathostatic [[Entity 8|Egremond Syndrome]], and a number of former [[Party Crashers]] with jaundiced, leprotic skin due to pathostatic [[Sanguine Festivus]].Columbus, Jacob (Director) ''et al.'', 2024. ''A Leper's Life: Half a Jovial'' [Documentary]. [[Party Crashers|Killjoy Administrative Productions]]. As may be expected, pathostasis is easily considered one of the most fascinating forms of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' amongst the medical community. Studies of pathostatic [[Entity 8|Egremond Syndrome]] have shown that the viruses in the patient's afflicted leg are killed off almost instantly once they exit the leg via the bloodstream or any other means, keeping the rest of the body free of the infection; as white blood cell activity is not documented to be more frequent in those areas, an explanation cannot yet be offered for this anomalous phenomenon. The same occurs with diseases such as [[Sanguine Festivus]], [[Carpetvirus]], [[Hydrolitis Plague]] and [[Entity 10|Capgras Delusion]]Gardien, Erik (2022). "The Characteristics of a Pathostatic Infection". ''The Xeniatrologist'', 9(5). — in the latter case, any blood flowing through the infected area loses its pigment, only for its haemoglobin to be anomalously replenished upon exiting. An individual with pathostasis is still able to control their infected body parts, although in severe cases the condition may lead to paralysis, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Necrosis necrosis] or the formation of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cancer cancerous tumors].Musgrave, Jeremy R. (2020). "Complications of Pathostasis". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 29(7). A noteworthy example of a person affected by xenostasis is acclaimed xeniatrologist [[Asclepius Ang|Dr Asclepius L. Ang]], whose pathostatic [[Entity 10|Capgras Delusion]] in the leg, amygdala and part of the limbic system has caused multiple shifts in his personality, as well as the ability to transform the shape of his leg until it became paralysed.[[Asclepius Ang|Ang, Asclepius L.]] (2022). ''Candy ''Canes''? Are You Mocking Me?'' Anemoiacore.Bucket, Charles (2025). ''The Capgras Doctor''. Greyscale House.
'''Thermoligohydria'''
{| style="width: 100%; margin-top:1em; text-align:left; border-radius: 1px;" |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Other names''' | style="width: 70%;" | Mimosa Syndrome; raisin-pox |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Specialty''' | style="width: 70%;" | Dermatology, endocrinology, nephrology, physiology, xeniatrology |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Symptoms''' | style="width: 70%;" | Severe dehydration on exposure to high temperatures or pressure |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Complications''' | style="width: 70%;" | Shrivelling, hypovolemic shock, kidney failure |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Diagnostic method''' | style="width: 70%;" | Based on symptoms, blood testing, urinalysis |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Deaths''' | style="width: 70%;" | Common |}
=== Thermoligohydria === '''Thermoligohydria''',Derived from ''thermo-'' ("pertaining to heat"), ''oligo-'' ("few") and Ancient Greek ''ὕδωρ'' ("water"). also known as '''Mimosa Syndrome''' or informally as '''raisin-pox''',This is a misnomer, as thermoligohydria is not a viral infection but an anomalous condition. is an Anatomical Anomaly which causes the body, when experiencing high temperatures, to rapidly lose water and appear shrivelled as a result; application of pressure to a certain part of the affected individual's anatomy can also trigger loss of water and subsequent desiccation in that specific location. Rapid dehydration can be precipitated by temperatures as low as 25°C, although most cases are only prompted at around 31°C;Falloman, Beatrice (2019). "Thermoligohydria". ''The Xeniatrologist'', 6(7). afflicted individuals are strongly advised to stay in colder, less humid levels (e.g. [[Level 4]]). Interestingly, water taken from the bodies of people afflicted with Mimosa Syndrome has been proven to contain trace amounts of a non-harmful strain of cyanide, making laboratory tests the most effective way to diagnose the condition.Amygdalum, Russel ''et al.'' (2020). "Non-Harmful Cyanide Potentially Causing Thermoligohydria". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 29(2). Thermoligohydria is commonly stated to be one of the most dangerous forms of ''Anomaliae Corporis'', as the sudden and extreme depletion of water from the body may cause complications such as kidney failure. Notable cases of thermoligohydria include the late [[I.R.I.S.]] agent Mohammed al-Barazi, who was found by operatives from the [[Backrooms Health Organisation]] lying prostrate and extremely shrivelled on the ground of the [[Level 22|Parking Hub]];Reighnolds, Daniel (2024, April 11). "I.R.I.S. Agent Found Dead in Level 22!" [[Backrooms Today|''Backrooms, Today!'']]Chirkova, Miroslava (2024, May 30). "The Dangers of Mimosa Syndrome Are Real!" ''Dimensional''. as well as 20-year-old wanderer Maud Springerton, who checked into a [[Backrooms Red Cross|Red Cross]] field hospital with her entire arm withered, apparently as a result of an encounter with an [[Entity 16|undying]].Gupta, Advaita (2020). "Middle-Aged Woman with Shrivelled Arm Checks into Red Cross Hospital!" ''The Back Alley''.
'''Xoticopia'''
{| style="width: 100%; margin-top:1em; text-align:left; border-radius: 1px;" |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Other names''' | style="width: 70%;" | Elfeye, eagle-eye |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Specialty''' | style="width: 70%;" | Ophthamology, xeniatrology |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Symptoms''' | style="width: 70%;" | Heightened visual perception, resistance to eye injuries |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Complications''' | style="width: 70%;" | Hypermetropia, photophobia |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Diagnostic method''' | style="width: 70%;" | Based on symptoms, ophthalmoscopy |- | style="padding: 4px; width: 30%; | '''Deaths''' | style="width: 70%;" | Rare |}
=== Xoticopia === '''Xoticopia''',Derived from Greek ''ξωτικό'' ("elf") and ''-opia'' (forming the name of ocular conditions), in reference to the remarkable eyesight of Tolkien's elves. also known informally as '''elfeye''' or '''eagle's eye''', is an Anatomical Anomaly which makes the eye extremely sensitive to light, serving to heighten the visual capabilities of an afflicted individual immensely.Johnstone, Michael (2017) [2011]. ''Elfeye''. Egremond & Roth. Xoticopiacs have been known to display a higher resistance to photophobia as well as external eye injuries, although studies have also shown that the condition may eventually cause hypermetropia to develop. Individuals with xoticopia report sharp vision, being able to see faraway objects with extreme clarity, as well as being able to plainly perceive articles in the midst of oppressive darkness.Superbus, Tarquin K. (Director) ''et al.'', 2022. ''A Day in the Life of the Anomalous'' [Documentary]. Foremost Films. The most common method for diagnosis of xoticopia is through ophthalmoscopy, which reveals an anomalous silver circlet around the iris, similar to the copper-coloured [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kayser%E2%80%93Fleischer_ring Kayser-Fleischer rings] found around irises of patients affected by [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wilson%27s_disease Wilson's disease].Gale, Dorothy (2016). "Xoticopia". ''The Xeniatrologist'', 3(10). The An Apple a Day medicinal department of the [[M.E.G.]] has been studying this phenomenon to determine its cause, and have discovered that these rings are in fact deposits of actual silver in the eyes.Ivanovich, Johann ''et al.'' (2023). "Silver Eyes: A Study of Xoticopia". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 32(8). An example of a xoticopic individual is the Private Brooklyn Rhinestone of the [[Party Crashers]], whose enhanced vision has been stated to be of great importance during scouting missions.Reighnolds, Daniel (2025, November 30). "Interview with the Party Crashers: What's Behind Those Masks?" [[Backrooms Today|''Backrooms, Today!'']] == Causes == Potential causes of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' are currently being researched under the Anomalia Awareness Research Initiative. At present, the leading hypothesis is that the phenomenon is [[Phenomenon 9|tulpaic]], caused by psychic radiation bleeding into reality and thus altering the anatomy of individuals; according to the theory, Anatomical Anomalies only afflict individuals who have just entered the Backrooms, either by [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/No-Clipping?redirect=no no-clippage] or upon birth, because that is when their bodies are most vulnerable to psychic radiation — no-clippage, especially in interdimensional instances, leaves the anatomy in a weakened and discombobulated state for several minutes, and other than this the most susceptible human beings to Achospic emissions are foeti.Rivers, Ethan P. (2016). ''The Wretched Cycle and How It Works''. Greyscale House. At all other times, the human body is either too robust to be warped in this way by the radiation, or else so grievously damaged that it dies before symptoms of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' have the chance to present themselves. == Research == Research of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' was severely lacking amongst the medical community until the early 21st century. In March 2014, the [[Backrooms Health Organisation|Backrooms Health Practitioner Authority]] put the Anomalia Awareness Research Initiative (A.A.R.I.) into motion as a subset of the [[Backrooms Health Organisation|Liminal Health and Wellbeing Initiative]],Diggs, Charlotte (2014). "Kindling the Fire: The Beginning of a Collective Study into ''Anomaliae Corporis''". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 26(5). sparking a period of intensive research on the phenomenon that was brought to an abrupt end in 2016 by the events of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]].Ruxton, Peter (2016, May 26). "The Flicker: Let's Commemorate What We Lost". ''The New Londoner''. In August 2020, a reinitiation of the A.A.R.I. was orchestrated by the [[Backrooms Red Cross]]; the organisation introduced Xeniatrological Research Unit 303 (XRU-303) to their Department of Biomedical Research, which would be dedicated to the research of potentially dangerous strains of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' including cephalorrheximegaly and cryexia.Prime, Octavius (2021, August 9). "Bringing Back the A.A.R.I. — Xeniatrologists Rejoice!" [[Backrooms Red Cross|''The Scarlet Crucifixion'']]. Other groups under the B.H.P.A. also began to introduce research teams dedicated to ''Anomaliae Corporis'', including the [[Backrooms Health Organisation|B.H.O.]]'s Research Department of Anatomical Anomalies for general investigation of the phenomenon;Cracker, Graham (2021, November 23). "Establishment of the Research Department of Anatomical Anomalies". [[Backrooms Health Organisation|''The Backrooms Health Newsletter'']]. the [[M.E.G.]]'s Apple of the Eye medical unit for the study of xoticopia;Triste, Jeremiah S. D. (2022, January 15). "Apple of the Eye: A New Medical Research Team". ''The New Londonder''. and the [[Level 4|S.R.C.]]'s Mantis Ward for the research of encephalomantiasis, while also providing therapeutic assistance for the cerebrally psychostatic.Sallow, Godfrey H. (2021, October 29). "Introducing the Mantis Ward!" ''Red Cross Societal''. The collective main goal of these research teams is to discover a treatment for ''Anomaliae Corporis''. Although little of the actual conclusions drawn from the A.A.R.I. have been publicly announced, the Board of Directors of B.H.P.A. has assured the public that much progress has been made toward comprehending this phenomenon and developing a cure for the conditions it comprises. In 2021, it was announced that a total of three official treatises would be published on the subject matter, discussing its effects, causes, and finally its treatment;Diggs, Charlotte (2021). "The Fire Still Smoulders: An Update on the Anomalia Research Initiative". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 30(11).Grierson, Robert D. ''et al.'' (2021, December 23). "No, They Haven't Given Up on the A.A.R.I. (and No One Thought They Did)!" ''The Back Alley''. since then, one of these dissertations has already been published under the title of ''The Effects of Anatomical Anomalies in Their Variably Benign or Malignant Forms: Part One of a Tripartite Xeniatrological Study'', written by acclaimed Wakeborn xeniatrologist [[Asclepius Ang|Dr Asclepius Ang]] of the [[Backrooms Red Cross|Red Cross]]. The second treatise, yet untitled, is set to be published in late 2026.Reighnolds, Daniel (2024, May 12). "Capgras Doctor Gives an Update on His Three-Part Treatise". [[Backrooms Today|''Backrooms, Today!'']] == In non-human entities == === [[Level 107|Cascadans]] === There have been account of Cascadans displaying symptoms of thermoligohydria, pathostasis and xoticopia, although in-depth research on the effects of ''Anomaliae Corporis'' on their anatomy has yet to be conducted. === [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cat Cats] === Some cats in the Backrooms have been known to display symptoms of xoticopia. Although most felines have superior low-light vision, the majority of cats are nearsighted and cannot clearly see objects more than around 6 metres away; however, there have been several documented cases in which cats have been able to perceive articles over 40 metres away from itself without much trouble. A recent case study, an anosmic American stray cat named Tomas who can detect a rat's corpse from 35 metres away, has revealed that xoticopic cats also possess that strange ring of silver around their irises, and that these rings become noticeably more discernable when eyeshine occurs.Martinez, C. Felicia (2024). "Xoticopia in Non-Primates: A Recent Case Study". ''The Wake Medical Journal'', 33(4).McCloven, Ian T. (2024, September 6). "Elves Come in Many Forms: Meet Tomas, the Feline Legolas". ''The New Londoner''. === [[Entity 9|Facelings]] === In 1985, a circus troupe of faceling performers called the Ring Ouroboros was established, and among its many flamboyant acts was the Amazing Bighead: a [[Entity 9|faceling]] whose head was swelled to immense size due to a case of cephalorrheximegaly.[[Entity_9|Wolfe, Atticus N.]] (2022) [2004]. ''The Famous Faceless: Facelings Who Matched Humanity''. Oligarchus.Kong, Kingsley W. J. (2016). ''That's Entertainment! The Uncanny Side of the Arts''. Anemoiacore. Later on, it is chronicled that the Bighead's head burst in the middle of a performance in late 1986, an event which Ringmaster Urobo expressed great regret for, as he had intended the Bighead to be an especial attraction in [[Maria|their upcoming act for Tobias M. Holdings]].Agtect, Agrius E. (2025). ''The Letters of Tobias M. Holdings''. [[User:Accountus|Accountus-Gamma]]. In addition to this, several cases of facelings afflicted with thermoligohydria, cryexia and hyperlongevitism have surfaced in the past few decades. === [[Entity 70|''Homo callidus'']] === Cavern art created by ''Homo callidus'' in sub-levels of the [[Level 8|Cave System]] has depicted both headless figures and figures with abnormally large heads,Dyte, Troggler (2019, October 12). "Peculiar figures in ancient ''Homo callidus'' art." ''Fossil''. which archaeologists interpreted as a sign that cephalorrheximegaly has been afflicting wanderers of the Backrooms since before the dawn of humanity.Kirke, F. Urwen (2019, September 19). "Cave Art Depicts Bigheads — How Far Back Does Cephalorrheximegaly Go?" [[Backrooms Red Cross|''The Scarlet Crucifixion'']]. Additionally, certain artworks show a single exiguously-clad individual amongst a group clothed in thick furs, perhaps a depiction of the effects of cryexia.Hastings, Liam W. (2021, April 9). "Dancing in the Snow: What's Up with That Guy?" ''The Gazette Twenty-Ninth''.Mann, Otto (2022). "Cryexia in Early Hominids".
